author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
tumi sarvaguṇayuta, śakti tava vaśībhūta,
vadānya, sarala, śuci, dhīra
dayālu, madhura, sama, kṛtī, sthira, sarvottama,
kṛtajña⋅lakṣaṇe punaḥ vīra

samasta kalyāṇa⋅guṇa⋅, gaṇāmṛta⋅sambhāvana,
samudra⋅svarūpa bhagavān
bindu bindu guṇa tava, sarvajīva⋅suvaibhava,
tumi pūrṇa sarvaśaktimān

e bhaktivinoda chāra, kṛtāñjali bāra bāra,
kare cittakathā vijñāpana
tava dāsagaṇa⋅saṅge, tava līlākathā⋅raṅge,
jāya jena āmāra jīvana
en_translation:
O Hari! You are endowed with all good qualities, and all power is under Your control. You are generous, simple, pure, steadfast, merciful, sweet, impartial, successful, unwavering, and supreme. You are valiant and possess a grateful nature.

O Lord, You are the ocean-like embodiment of all auspiciousness and nectar-like virtues! Every drop of Your qualities is the supreme wealth for all living entities. You are complete and all-powerful.

This insignificant Bhaktivinoda, with folded hands again and again, makes this heartfelt request: May my life pass in the company of Your servants, in the joy of the narratives of Your divine pastimes.
